sections.recommendation:
Adopt nine Resolutions authorizing the City Manager to approve contracts in response
to the April 24, 2025 Request for Qualifications, Specification No. 25-11721-C, “On-Call
Equitable Community Engagement Services with City of Berkeley,” with the following
firms, in not-to-exceed amounts of $250,000 each, for total contract periods of five
years:
 360 Total Concept Consulting
 Be the Change Consulting
 Community Planning Collaborative
 D & A Communications, Inc.
 Denaya Shorter Consulting
 The Ecology Center
 Equity First Consulting
 Moore Iacofano Goltsman, Incorporated (MIG)
 Plan to Place
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The selected consultants will form a list of on-call consultants to provide services to the
City when needed, on a task-order basis for specific projects. These projects may be
funded in a variety of ways, including state grants for community planning efforts, taxes
such as Measure FF, other special funds, or one-time General Fund allocations for City
Council priorities and strategic plan projects.
When a project requires outside community engagement services, City staff will forward
a request for services to at least three of the on-call consultants under contract,
including a project description and pertinent supporting information. The consultants will
respond with a proposed scope of work estimating the time required for the various
tasks, and their proposed cost for the specific project, based on the hourly rates
previously submitted in response to the Request for Qualifications (RFQ). The City will
review the proposals, select a consultant for the project, and negotiate a schedule and

not-to-exceed cost. A Notice to Proceed can then be issued with the full scope of work
and cost identified.
Each contractor may be paid up to its specified not-to-exceed amount over a five-year
period. Larger project amounts are reserved for contractors which can be called upon to
provide a wider range of services (e.g., project specific public meeting services, direct
payments to community focus group participants, technical support, or videography and
web services).
Proposals have included anticipated cost adjustments during the contract term. Upon
mutual agreement, contracts may be extended for two years. Contracts will be overseen
by the Planning and Development Department, but consultants may be engaged t
sections.current_situation:
Effective and intentional community engagement is critically important for City plans and
projects, particularly when working with community members who are most impacted or
have been historically underserved. The City’s On-Call Equitable Community
Engagement Services RFQ aims to create opportunities for staff to partner with a
diverse group of consultants and community organizations to expand and deepen the
City’s engagement efforts. This will help ensure that community members are included
in decision-making, that the City understands community goals, and that shared
priorities are reflected in future City work. Examples of recent projects which have
retained outside engagement services include:
 the creation of a new Environmental Justice Element for the General Plan and
update to the existing Safety Element of the General Plan, which includes
funding from the State of California’s Office of Land Use and Climate Innovation;
 the new Community Health Assessment and Implementation Plan, which utilized
a Future of Public Health grant; and
 the Pilot Climate Equity Fund Program which was seeded by a General Fund
budget referral.
For this RFQ, the City solicited written proposals from qualified firms, community-based
organizations, nonprofit organizations and individuals to assist with equitably engaging
the community, particularly those who are adversely impacted or historically
disadvantaged, on various City efforts.
The RFQ was released on April 24, 2025 and closed on June 24, 2025. The City
received 13 submittals, but one mistakenly submitted by a subcontractor was
withdrawn. The remaining 12 submittals were reviewed by a panel of City staff from
multiple departments.
Page 3 of 13
Contracts: On-Call Equitable Community Engagement Services CONSENT CALENDAR
January 27, 2026
Of the 12 proposals evaluated, 11 were selected to move forward to interviews and
reference checks. Following panel interviews with City staff, nine consultants were
selected as the most quali
sections.background:
Community outreach is a long-standing City obligation, particularly to communities who
are most impacted or have been historically underserved in City plans and projects.
City Council highlighted the ongoing need to elevate staff efforts in the development of
equitable community engagement when it adopted Resolution No. 69,501-N.S. to
“Transform Community Safety and Initiate a Robust Community Engagement Process”
on July 14, 2020, Resolution No. 71,582-N.S. recognizing the vision and goals of the
Equity for Black Berkeley (E4BB) on November 19, 2024, and a referral on December 2,
2025 titled, “Notifying Residents of Upcoming Public Works Projects in their
Neighborhoods.”
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The work of the selected on-call contractors will help ensure that City projects and new
infrastructure that impact local and regional climate resilience are reflective of the needs
and values of Berkley communities. Utilizing local and regional contractors for
community outreach also lowers vehicle miles traveled.
sections.rationale:
The lengthy process for Request for Proposals, RFQs, and contracts can delay projects
and make it more difficult for contractors to schedule their work. Having an on-call
contract already in place provides City departments with the flexibility to engage
consultants quickly, avoiding delays and helping to meet requirements set forth in many
of the City’s local, state, and federal grants and statutory outreach requirements. These
on-call contracts will expedite service delivery for the public and allow for more effective
planning processes for staff.
Further, these outside services are needed due to the specialized nature of community
outreach work, and the need to effectively serve members of the community who have
been historically underserved. 360 Total Concept Consulting, Be the Change
Consulting, Community Planning Collaborative, D & A Communications, Inc., Denaya
Shorter Consulting, The Ecology Center, Equity First Consulting, Moore Iacofano
Page 4 of 13
Contracts: On-Call Equitable Community Engagement Services CONSENT CALENDAR
January 27, 2026
Goltsman Incorporated, and Plan to Place are highly qualified and have particular
expertise in fulfilling the criteria stated in the RFQs.
sections.alternatives:
The City could continue to have individual departments contract separately for similar
services, as has been done previously.
CONTACT PERSONS
Kellie Knox, Community Services Specialist III, Health Housing and Community
Services (510) 981-5301
Amanda Montez, Senior Management Analyst, Public Works (510) 981-6317
Katie Van Dyke, Chief Resilience Officer, Planning and Development Department (510)
981-7403

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: 360 TOTAL CONCEPT CONSULTING FOR EQUITABLE COMMUNITY
ENGAGEMENT SERVICES
WHEREAS, when the City develops plans, projects, programs and/or policies it is critical
to have active, effective, and intentional community engagement – particularly with
community members who are most impacted and/or have been historically underserved;
and
WHEREAS, the City has contracted with Community Engagement consultants on an
ongoing basis for over 20 years to assist with equitably engaging the community,
particularly adversely impacted and/or historically disadvantaged members of the
community, on various City projects, programs, and/or policies; and
WHEREAS, these outside services are needed due to limited staff resources, the need
for additional staff training and the need to outreach to community members outside of
regular City staffing hours; and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley released a Request for Qualifications (RFQ)
(Specification No. 25-11721-C) on April 24, 2025, and closed on June 24, 2025; and
WHEREAS, staff reviewed the proposals and determined that 360 Total Concept
Consulting is well qualified to provide services that are needed and responsive to the
City’s request; and
WHEREAS, no work will be authorized under the contract and no funds will be
encumbered unless a department approves a Statement of Work and provides full
funding; and
WHEREAS, funding will be identified and requested for appropriation, if needed,
